propositions: When main advances, signed plan-review attestations read stale and each re-review of a byte-identical plan re-runs the full criteria set, exposing a measured 32% same-material verdict instability.
The progressive_drift_refresh config flag is being flipped from default False to default True so drift-only-stale re-reviews take the scoped E4+G1G2 grounding probe instead of a full re-review.
A drift-refresh probe runs only 2 agent-tier criteria (E4+G1G2) versus ~8-12 for a full review, yielding a ~75-83% reduction in agent-tier LLM calls with proportionate token/latency saving.
The probe refreshes the attestation when the plan still holds and fail-safes to a full re-review on probe error, registry-version skew, or no reusable prior verdict, preserving correctness.
Explicit progressive_drift_refresh = false must restore the full re-review path, and stale default-OFF framing must be swept from src/, tests/, and docs/ (dated ADR notes excepted).
The probe's criteria scope, the staling conditions, and Story B's Pass-2 grounding hardening are explicit non-goals.
